extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. The property G(opted_out implies not sent) requires that no reachable state has opted_out=true AND sent=true.

Transition t_resume_glitch fires on send_attempt when (opted_out AND ticks>=12) and sets sent:=true — directly producing the forbidden state, provided its guard is reachable. It is reachable:

1. Event 1 = optout: t_optout fires (guard true) -> opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2. Events 2-13 = twelve ticks: t_tick increments ticks (capped at 12) and sets sent:=false each time. After the 12th tick: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=12. (ticks>=12 now holds.)
3. Event 14 = send_attempt: with opted_out=true and ticks>=12, both t_send_blocked (guard opted_out) and t_resume_glitch (guard opted_out AND ticks>=12) are enabled. No priority is specified, so the choice is nondeterministic. An execution that takes t_resume_glitch sets sent:=true while opted_out=true, yielding state opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=12. This violates opted_out implies not sent.

The property is universally quantified over all reachable executions ("on every reachable execution"), so the existence of one violating nondeterministic choice falsifies it. The sequence uses 14 events, within the 16-event bound.

Thus the property is VIOLATED. Witness event sequence: optout, then 12 ticks, then send_attempt (resolving the send_attempt via t_resume_glitch).
